description: You are a detective investigating corporate espionage. A seized USB drive image must be structurally profiled to determine if the data collection was systematically organized. Create a forensic case in Autopsy named 'Directory_Profile_2024' with case number 'INV-DSP-007' and add /home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd as a Disk Image data source, enabling only the 'File Type Identification' ingest module. Generate a pipe-delimited directory tree listing at /home/ga/Reports/directory_tree.txt with a header row and the format: 'DEPTH|ENTRY_TYPE|NAME|FULL_PATH|SIZE_BYTES'. DEPTH is the nesting level (0 = root), and ENTRY_TYPE is 'DIR', 'FILE', 'DEL_FILE', or 'DEL_DIR'. Exclude virtual Autopsy nodes like '$CarvedFiles' or '$OrphanFiles', but include standard system and hidden files. Additionally, write an organizational profile to /home/ga/Reports/organization_profile.txt containing exactly these sections on separate lines: 'VOLUME_FILESYSTEM: <type>', 'TOTAL_DIRECTORIES: <N>', 'TOTAL_FILES: <N>', 'ALLOCATED_FILES: <N>', 'DELETED_FILES: <N>', 'MAX_DIRECTORY_DEPTH: <N>', 'FILE_EXTENSION_DISTRIBUTION:' (followed by lines formatted '  .<ext>: <N> files' sorted by count descending, using '<none>' for no extension), 'TOP_LEVEL_DIRECTORIES:' (followed by lines formatted '  <dirname>/ (N items)'), and 'ORGANIZATIONAL_ASSESSMENT: <systematic|flat|minimal>'. Both report files must follow these strict formatting requirements.
